station_card.title: Chest discomfort after lunch in a 58-year-old man
station_card.heading: CANDIDATE INFORMATION AND TASKS
candidate_information: You are working in a general practice clinic.
Your next patient is David Nguyen, aged 58 years.
He has come in because of chest discomfort that started after lunch.
tasks: Take a concise, relevant and focused history.
Ask the examiner for any examination findings or observations you require.
Explain your likely concerns to the patient.

opening_line.speaker: David
opening_line.text: Doctor, I think it is just indigestion, but my chest feels really tight.

reveal_actions.label: Pain story
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.candidate_request_prompt: Ask David about onset, activity, site, character, severity, radiation, and whether the pain is settling.
reveal_actions.response_text: It started about 45 minutes ago while I was carrying boxes. It is in the centre of my chest, heavy and tight, about 8 out of 10. It goes into my left arm and jaw, and it is not settling.

reveal_actions.label: Associated symptoms
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.candidate_request_prompt: Ask David about sweating, nausea, breathlessness, dizziness, and collapse.
reveal_actions.response_text: I feel sweaty and sick, and I am a bit short of breath. It is starting to scare me.

reveal_actions.label: Risk factors and background
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.candidate_request_prompt: After urgent transfer is underway, ask David for the focused cardiovascular background that changes risk.
reveal_actions.response_text: I smoke. I have high blood pressure and high cholesterol. My father had a heart attack.

reveal_actions.label: Ideas and concerns
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.candidate_request_prompt: Ask David what he thinks is happening and what is worrying him.
reveal_actions.response_text: I thought it was indigestion because it started after lunch. I am worried about missing work.

reveal_actions.label: Ask examiner for observations and examination
reveal_actions.speaker: Examiner
reveal_actions.candidate_request_prompt: Ask the examiner for David's general appearance, immediate observations, and focused cardiovascular and respiratory examination findings.
reveal_actions.response_text: David looks pale and sweaty. Heart rate 104, blood pressure 155/92, respiratory rate 22, oxygen saturation 95% on room air, temperature 36.8, pain 8/10. His chest is clear. There is no new murmur and no calf swelling.

reveal_actions.label: Ask examiner about available investigations
reveal_actions.speaker: Examiner
reveal_actions.candidate_request_prompt: Ask the examiner whether an ECG and troponin result are available in this consultation.
reveal_actions.response_text: A 12-lead ECG can be performed in the clinic. A troponin result is not available within this consultation.

reveal_actions.label: Response to your plan
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.candidate_request_prompt: Explain your concern and management plan to David, then listen to his response.
reveal_actions.response_text: An ambulance? Can I just drive myself? Is it really that serious?

plan_gate.label: I have discussed my plan.
plan_gate.candidate_prompt: Explain your concern and urgent plan aloud before using this gate.
